title: Quiz: Fisher Information
description: Test your understanding of classical and quantum Fisher information, SLD formalism, and multiparameter estimation

questions.question: What is the classical Fisher information for a Gaussian distribution N(mu, sigma^2) with unknown mean mu?
questions.explanation: For N(mu, sigma^2), the Fisher information about mu is F = 1/sigma^2. This makes intuitive sense: smaller variance means each observation carries more information about the mean.

questions.question: The Symmetric Logarithmic Derivative (SLD) L_theta satisfies which equation?
questions.explanation: The SLD is defined by the symmetric equation d_rho/d_theta = (L*rho + rho*L)/2. This ensures L is Hermitian when rho is Hermitian, making it a valid observable.

questions.question: How is the Quantum Fisher Information related to the SLD?
questions.explanation: The QFI is defined as F_Q = Tr(rho * L^2), which is the expectation value of L^2 in state rho. This represents the variance of the SLD observable.

questions.question: When does the classical Fisher information equal the Quantum Fisher Information?

options.text: Always, they are the same quantity
When measuring in the computational basis
When measuring in the SLD eigenbasis

questions.explanation: The classical FI equals QFI when measuring in the eigenbasis of the Symmetric Logarithmic Derivative L. This is the optimal measurement that saturates the quantum Cramer-Rao bound.

questions.question: In multiparameter quantum estimation, the optimal measurement for one parameter is always compatible with the optimal measurement for another parameter.
questions.explanation: False! Parameters are compatible only when their SLDs commute: [L_i, L_j] = 0. When SLDs don't commute, there is no single measurement that optimally estimates both parameters simultaneously.

questions.question: What geometric structure does Fisher information define on the space of probability distributions?
questions.explanation: Fisher information defines the Fisher-Rao metric, a Riemannian metric on the statistical manifold of probability distributions. This is the unique metric invariant under sufficient statistics.

questions.question: For the QFI matrix element F_ij in multiparameter estimation, what is the formula?
questions.explanation: The QFI matrix element is F_ij = (1/2) * Tr(rho * {L_i, L_j}), where {A,B} = AB + BA is the anticommutator. This ensures the matrix is real and symmetric.

questions.question: Which statements about Fisher information are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.text: For independent observations, Fisher information is additive
Classical FI can exceed Quantum FI with the right measurement
QFI equals zero for a maximally mixed state

questions.explanation: (a) Correct - FI is a variance, hence non-negative. (b) Correct - n independent observations give n*F. (c) Incorrect - classical FI is always <= QFI. (d) Correct - maximally mixed state carries no information about parameters, so QFI = 0.
